message:
feat(wizard): add VPS provider comparison table (bd-w8fx)

Add a responsive comparison table to the "Rent a VPS" wizard step
showing Contabo and OVH side by side with specs, pricing, and
activation times.

- Create lib/vpsProviders.ts with structured provider data and
  pricing for 64GB and 48GB plan tiers
- Create components/wizard/VPSComparison.tsx with tier toggle,
  desktop table view, mobile card view, and tracked external links
- Integrate into rent-vps page between spec checklist and signup info

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"bd-td8y","title":"Deep exploration: Mail (MCP Agent Mail)","description":"## Goal
Perform deep exploration of MCP Agent Mail and revise its description on the flywheel/TLDR pages with comprehensive testing and validation.

## Phase 0: Pre-flight Verification (CRITICAL)

### 0.1 Tool Existence Check
```bash
#!/bin/bash
LOG=/tmp/mail-preflight.log
echo "=== Mail Pre-flight Check: $(date) ===" | tee $LOG

# Verify tool directory exists
if [[ -d /dp/mcp_agent_mail ]]; then
  echo "PASS: /dp/mcp_agent_mail directory exists" | tee -a $LOG
else
  echo "FAIL: /dp/mcp_agent_mail NOT FOUND - aborting" | tee -a $LOG
  exit 1
fi

# Verify README exists
[[ -f /dp/mcp_agent_mail/README.md ]] && echo "PASS: README.md exists" || echo "WARN: README.md not found"

# Check if MCP server is configured
grep -r "mcp-agent-mail" ~/.claude/settings.json 2>/dev/null && echo "PASS: MCP server configured" || echo "WARN: MCP server may not be configured"
```

### 0.2 Content Snapshot (BEFORE State)
```bash
#!/bin/bash
SNAPSHOT_DIR=/tmp/mail-exploration-snapshots
mkdir -p $SNAPSHOT_DIR
cp /data/projects/agentic_coding_flywheel_setup/apps/web/lib/flywheel.ts $SNAPSHOT_DIR/flywheel.ts.before
cp /data/projects/agentic_coding_flywheel_setup/apps/web/lib/tldr-content.ts $SNAPSHOT_DIR/tldr-content.ts.before
grep -A 100 "id: 'mail'" /data/projects/agentic_coding_flywheel_setup/apps/web/lib/flywheel.ts | head -100 > $SNAPSHOT_DIR/mail-flywheel-entry.before.txt
echo "Snapshots saved to $SNAPSHOT_DIR"
```

### 0.3 Verify TypeScript Interfaces
```bash
grep -A 30 "export interface FlywheelTool" /data/projects/agentic_coding_flywheel_setup/apps/web/lib/flywheel.ts
grep -A 30 "export interface TldrFlywheelTool" /data/projects/agentic_coding_flywheel_setup/apps/web/lib/tldr-content.ts
```

## Phase 1: Research (GATHER CONTEXT)

### 1.1 Primary Documentation
```bash
wc -l /dp/mcp_agent_mail/README.md
cat /dp/mcp_agent_mail/README.md
ls -la /dp/mcp_agent_mail/docs/ 2>/dev/null
cat /dp/mcp_agent_mail/AGENTS.md 2>/dev/null
```

### 1.2 Code Investigation
- Launch code investigation agent to understand:
  - FastMCP server architecture and tool registration
  - SQLite storage schema (messages, agents, file_reservations, agent_links)
  - Advisory file reservation system (exclusive vs shared, TTL, force release)
  - Message threading, FTS5 search, summarization
  - All MCP tools: send_message, reply_message, fetch_inbox, mark_message_read, acknowledge_message
  - File reservation tools: file_reservation_paths, release_file_reservations, renew_file_reservations
  - Agent tools: register_agent, create_agent_identity, whois
  - Macro helpers: macro_start_session, macro_prepare_thread, macro_file_reservation_cycle
  - Git archive persistence layer

### 1.3 External Context Search
```bash
/xf search 'agent mail OR mcp_agent_mail OR file_reservation' 2>&1 | head -50
cass search 'agent mail mcp coordination' --robot --limit 10 2>&1
cass search 'file reservation lock' --robot --limit 5 2>&1
```

### 1.4 Project State Review
```bash
ls -la /dp/mcp_agent_mail/.beads/ 2>/dev/null
cd /dp/mcp_agent_mail && git log --oneline -20 && cd -
find /dp/mcp_agent_mail -name "*.md" -type f 2>/dev/null | head -10
```

### 1.5 MCP Tool Inventory
```bash
# List actual MCP tools from source
grep -r "def.*(" /dp/mcp_agent_mail/src/*.py 2>/dev/null | grep -v "__" | head -30
# Or check the tool registrations
grep -r "@mcp\|tool_name\|register" /dp/mcp_agent_mail/src/*.py 2>/dev/null | head -30
```

## Phase 2: Analysis (SYNTHESIZE UNDERSTANDING)

Document findings:
- [ ] Core messaging: send_message, reply_message, fetch_inbox verified
- [ ] Agent identity: register_agent, whois verified
- [ ] File reservations: exclusive/shared modes, TTL, force release verified
- [ ] Threading: thread_id handling verified
- [ ] Search: FTS5 query syntax verified
- [ ] Macros: macro_start_session use cases verified
- [ ] Synergies VERIFIED:
  - [ ] bv: [does bv read mail messages?]
  - [ ] cm: [does cm use mail?]
  - [ ] slb: [how does slb use mail?]
  - [ ] ntm: [how does ntm coordinate via mail?]
- [ ] Tech stack verified: Python, FastMCP, SQLite, Pydantic
- [ ] Known limitations discovered

## Phase 3: Revision (UPDATE DESCRIPTIONS)

### 3.1 Update apps/web/lib/flywheel.ts
```typescript
{
  id: 'mail',
  name: 'MCP Agent Mail',
  shortName: 'Mail',
  href: string,  // valid GitHub URL
  icon: LucideIcon,
  color: string,
  tagline: string,  // e.g., "Message-passing coordination for multi-agent workflows"
  description: string,
  deepDescription: string,  // include FastMCP, SQLite, file reservations
  connectsTo: string[],  // VERIFIED synergies only
  connectionDescriptions: Record<string, string>,
  stars: number,
  features: string[],  // VERIFIED: 20+ MCP tools, file reservations, threading
  cliCommands: [
    { command: 'mcp__mcp-agent-mail__send_message', description: '...' },
    { command: 'mcp__mcp-agent-mail__file_reservation_paths', description: '...' },
    // ... all VERIFIED tools
  ],
  installCommand: string,
  language: 'Python',
}
```

### 3.2 Update apps/web/lib/tldr-content.ts
- Verify all fields match TldrFlywheelTool interface
- whatItDoes: accurate messaging + file reservation description
- synergies: VERIFIED connections only

### 3.3 Cross-reference Validation
```bash
# Verify reciprocal synergies
for tool in bv cm slb ntm; do
  if grep -A10 "id: '$tool'" /data/projects/agentic_coding_flywheel_setup/apps/web/lib/flywheel.ts | grep -q "mail"; then
    echo "PASS: $tool lists mail"
  else
    echo "WARN: $tool does NOT list mail"
  fi
done
```

### 3.4 De-slopify
- Remove: "harness", "empower", "leverage", "robust", "seamless"
- Verify no placeholder text remains

## Phase 4: Testing (VERIFY QUALITY)

### 4.1 Static Analysis
```bash
#!/bin/bash
LOG=/tmp/mail-static-analysis.log
cd /data/projects/agentic_coding_flywheel_setup/apps/web
echo "=== Mail Static Analysis ===" | tee $LOG
bun run type-check 2>&1 | tee -a $LOG; echo "Type Check: $?" | tee -a $LOG
bun run lint 2>&1 | tee -a $LOG; echo "Lint: $?" | tee -a $LOG
```

### 4.2 Build Verification
```bash
#!/bin/bash
LOG=/tmp/mail-build.log
cd /data/projects/agentic_coding_flywheel_setup/apps/web
bun run build 2>&1 | tee $LOG
if [[ $? -ne 0 ]]; then
  echo "FAIL: Build failed - restoring snapshot"
  cp /tmp/mail-exploration-snapshots/flywheel.ts.before lib/flywheel.ts
  cp /tmp/mail-exploration-snapshots/tldr-content.ts.before lib/tldr-content.ts
  exit 1
fi
```

### 4.3 E2E Visual Verification
```bash
#!/bin/bash
LOG=/tmp/mail-e2e.log
cd /data/projects/agentic_coding_flywheel_setup/apps/web

# Kill any existing dev server
lsof -t -i :3000 | xargs kill 2>/dev/null; sleep 2

bun run dev &
DEV_PID=$!
for i in {1..30}; do curl -s http://localhost:3000 &>/dev/null && break; sleep 1; done

curl -sL http://localhost:3000/flywheel | grep -qi 'mail' && echo "PASS: mail on /flywheel" || echo "FAIL"
curl -sL http://localhost:3000/tldr | grep -qi 'mail' && echo "PASS: mail on /tldr" || echo "FAIL"

kill $DEV_PID 2>/dev/null
```

### 4.4 Unit Tests: Content Integrity
```bash
#!/bin/bash
LOG=/tmp/mail-unit-tests.log
cd /data/projects/agentic_coding_flywheel_setup/apps/web
echo "=== Mail Content Tests ===" | tee $LOG

grep -q "id: 'mail'" lib/flywheel.ts && echo "PASS: mail entry exists" || echo "FAIL"
grep -q "send_message\|file_reservation" lib/flywheel.ts && echo "PASS: MCP tools mentioned" || echo "FAIL"
grep -q "id: 'mail'" lib/tldr-content.ts && echo "PASS: mail in tldr" || echo "FAIL"

# Verify content changed
diff -q lib/flywheel.ts /tmp/mail-exploration-snapshots/flywheel.ts.before && echo "WARN: No changes" || echo "PASS: Content updated"
```

### 4.5 MCP Tool Verification
```bash
# Verify documented MCP tools actually exist by checking if they're available
# This requires the MCP server to be running
echo "MCP tools to verify:"
echo "- send_message"
echo "- reply_message"  
echo "- file_reservation_paths"
echo "- release_file_reservations"
# Manual verification required with actual MCP calls
```

### 4.6 Consolidated Test Results
```bash
#!/bin/bash
RESULTS=/tmp/mail-exploration-test-results.log
echo "=== MAIL DEEP EXPLORATION RESULTS ===" > $RESULTS
echo "Date: $(date)" >> $RESULTS
grep -E "PASS|FAIL|WARN" /tmp/mail-*.log >> $RESULTS 2>/dev/null
FAIL_COUNT=$(grep -c "FAIL" $RESULTS)
echo "FAILURES: $FAIL_COUNT" >> $RESULTS
cat $RESULTS
```

## Phase 5: Commit (FINALIZE)

### 5.1 Pre-commit Check
```bash
[[ $(grep -c "FAIL" /tmp/mail-exploration-test-results.log) -gt 0 ]] && echo "ABORT: Failures" && exit 1
```

### 5.2 Commit
```bash
cd /data/projects/agentic_coding_flywheel_setup
git add apps/web/lib/flywheel.ts apps/web/lib/tldr-content.ts
git commit -m "docs(flywheel): update Agent Mail descriptions with verified MCP tools

- Verified 20+ MCP tools against actual implementation
- Documented file reservation system accurately
- Verified synergies (not assumed)
- Passed all tests

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>"

br update bd-td8y --status closed
br sync --flush-only && git add .beads/
git push
```

### 5.3 Rollback if needed
```bash
cp /tmp/mail-exploration-snapshots/*.before apps/web/lib/
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Pre-flight passed (tool exists)
- [ ] Content snapshot captured
- [ ] All tests PASS (zero FAIL)
- [ ] MCP tools VERIFIED against implementation
- [ ] File reservation system accurately documented
- [ ] Synergies VERIFIED (not assumed)
- [ ] Reciprocal synergies confirmed
- [ ] Content de-slopified
- [ ] Pushed to remote
- [ ] Bead closed

-{"id":"bd-w8fx","title":"VPS provider comparison table in web wizard","description":"## Overview
Add a VPS provider comparison table in the web wizard to help beginners choose between Hetzner, DigitalOcean, Vultr, etc.

## Current Problem
- Users must research providers externally
- No guidance on which provider fits their needs
- Price/performance tradeoffs unclear
- "Which one should I pick?" is common question

## Proposed Comparison Table
| Provider | Cheapest VPS | RAM | Storage | Best For |
|----------|-------------|-----|---------|----------|
| Hetzner | $4.50/mo | 4GB | 40GB | Best value |
| DigitalOcean | $6/mo | 1GB | 25GB | Simplicity |
| Vultr | $5/mo | 1GB | 25GB | Global locations |
| Linode | $5/mo | 1GB | 25GB | Good support |

## Data to Show
- Starting price (monthly)
- Specs at price point (RAM, CPU, storage)
- Data center locations
- Key differentiator
- Referral link (if applicable)

## Implementation Details
1. Create `VPSComparison` component
2. Store provider data in `lib/vpsProviders.ts`
3. Sortable/filterable table
4. "Recommended" badge for Hetzner (best value)
5. External links open in new tab

## Affiliate Disclosure
- Clear disclosure if referral links used
- Option to use direct links instead
- Transparency builds trust

## Keep Data Fresh
- Provider data in separate config file
- Easy to update prices without code changes
- Last updated timestamp shown

## Test Plan
- [ ] Test table renders all providers
- [ ] Test sorting by price, RAM, etc.
- [ ] Test external links work
- [ ] Test mobile responsive layout
- [ ] Accessibility: screen reader compatible

## Files to Create
- apps/web/components/wizard/VPSComparison.tsx
- apps/web/lib/vpsProviders.ts (provider data)
- Modify: VPS selection wizard step","status":"in_progress","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-25T23:02:10.332520326Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-26T22:47:17.945380788Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-w8fx","depends_on_id":"bd-3y1n","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-25T23:04:18.677662781Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

 {"id":"bd-w8iz","title":"Deep exploration: CM (CASS Memory System)","description":"## Goal
Perform deep exploration of CM (CASS Memory System) and revise its description on the flywheel/TLDR pages with comprehensive testing.

## Phase 0: Pre-flight Verification (CRITICAL)

### 0.1 Tool Existence Check
```bash
# Verify CM installation
[[ -d /dp/cass_memory_system ]] && echo "PASS: cm repo exists" || { echo "FAIL: cm repo missing"; exit 1; }
command -v cm &>/dev/null && echo "PASS: cm command available" || { echo "FAIL: cm not in PATH"; exit 1; }

# Verify cass dependency (cm depends on cass)
command -v cass &>/dev/null && echo "PASS: cass available (dependency)" || echo "WARN: cass not installed"
```

### 0.2 Content Snapshot (BEFORE State)
```bash
SNAPSHOT_DIR=/tmp/cm-exploration-snapshots-$(date +%Y%m%d-%H%M%S)
mkdir -p $SNAPSHOT_DIR
cp apps/web/lib/flywheel.ts $SNAPSHOT_DIR/flywheel.ts.before
cp apps/web/lib/tldr-content.ts $SNAPSHOT_DIR/tldr-content.ts.before
echo "Snapshots saved to $SNAPSHOT_DIR"
```

### 0.3 TypeScript Interface Reference
Content must match FlywheelTool interface:
- id, name, tagline, description, deepDescription
- features[], cliCommands[], connectsTo[]
- integrationLevel, category, status

## Phase 1: Research (GATHER CONTEXT)

### 1.1 Primary Documentation
- `cat /dp/cass_memory_system/README.md` - Read full README
- Check for memory schema docs, playbook format specs

### 1.2 Code Investigation
- Launch code investigation agent to understand:
  - Memory storage format (JSON/YAML playbooks)
  - How it integrates with CASS for session mining
  - Playbook generation algorithm
  - Procedural memory patterns (triggers, responses)
  - /cm skill integration in Claude Code

### 1.3 CLI Command Verification
```bash
# Verify each documented command works
cm --help 2>&1 | head -20
cm recall --help 2>&1 | head -10
cm playbook --help 2>&1 | head -10

# Test actual functionality
cm list 2>&1 | head -5
```

### 1.4 External Context Search
- `/xf search 'cass memory OR cm OR procedural memory'` - Twitter archive
- `cass search 'cm memory playbook' --robot --limit 10` - Past sessions

### 1.5 Project State Review
- Check beads in /dp/cass_memory_system/.beads/
- Review recent commits: `cd /dp/cass_memory_system && git log --oneline -20`

## Phase 2: Analysis (SYNTHESIZE UNDERSTANDING)

### 2.1 Core Capabilities Verification
Document findings for each area:
- [ ] Memory storage format (file structure, schema)
- [ ] CASS integration mechanism (how it queries sessions)
- [ ] Playbook structure and generation (template format)
- [ ] Session recall capabilities (search, filter)
- [ ] Practical memory workflows

### 2.2 Synergy Verification
Cross-reference these tools actually integrate:
- [ ] cass - primary dependency for session mining
- [ ] mail - message integration
- [ ] ntm - multi-agent memory sharing

### 2.3 Claim Verification
```bash
# Verify memory storage location
ls -la ~/.local/share/cm/ 2>/dev/null || echo "No memory data yet"

# Verify playbook format
find ~/.local/share/cm -name "*.yaml" -o -name "*.json" 2>/dev/null | head -5
```

## Phase 3: Revision (UPDATE DESCRIPTIONS)

### 3.1 Update apps/web/lib/flywheel.ts
Update cm entry with VERIFIED information:
- `tagline`: Accurate one-liner from README
- `description`: Memory storage and retrieval
- `deepDescription`: How it builds playbooks from cass
- `features`: Verified memory capabilities
- `cliCommands`: Only commands that actually work
- `connectsTo`: Only verified integrations (must include cass)

### 3.2 Update apps/web/lib/tldr-content.ts
Update TldrFlywheelTool entry with:
- `briefDescription`: Technical summary
- `bulletPoints`: Verified capabilities
- `synergyExamples`: Working integration examples

## Phase 4: Testing (VERIFY CHANGES)

### 4.1 TypeScript Compilation
```bash
cd apps/web && npx tsc --noEmit 2>&1 | head -20
```

### 4.2 Unit Tests
```bash
# Test cm entry structure
node -e "
const { flywheelTools } = require('./lib/flywheel');
const cm = flywheelTools.find(t => t.id === 'cm');
console.log('Testing cm entry...');
console.assert(cm, 'cm entry exists');
console.assert(cm.features?.length > 0, 'has features');
console.assert(cm.connectsTo?.includes('cass'), 'connects to cass');
console.log('All assertions passed');
"
```

### 4.3 E2E Test: Memory Workflow
```bash
#\!/bin/bash
set -euo pipefail
LOG=/tmp/cm-e2e-$(date +%Y%m%d-%H%M%S).log

echo "=== CM E2E Test ===" | tee $LOG
echo "Started: $(date)" | tee -a $LOG

# Test 1: List memories
echo "Test 1: List memories..." | tee -a $LOG
cm list 2>&1 | tee -a $LOG

# Test 2: Recall capability
echo "Test 2: Recall test..." | tee -a $LOG
cm recall "testing" --limit 3 2>&1 | tee -a $LOG || echo "No matches (OK)"

# Test 3: Playbook generation (dry run if available)
echo "Test 3: Playbook check..." | tee -a $LOG
cm playbook --help 2>&1 | head -5 | tee -a $LOG

echo "=== All Tests Passed ===" | tee -a $LOG
echo "Log: $LOG"
```

### 4.4 Content Diff Verification
```bash
echo "=== Changes Made ==="
diff $SNAPSHOT_DIR/flywheel.ts.before apps/web/lib/flywheel.ts || true
diff $SNAPSHOT_DIR/tldr-content.ts.before apps/web/lib/tldr-content.ts || true
```

## Phase 5: Completion (FINALIZE)

### 5.1 Rollback Procedure (if tests fail)
```bash
cp $SNAPSHOT_DIR/flywheel.ts.before apps/web/lib/flywheel.ts
cp $SNAPSHOT_DIR/tldr-content.ts.before apps/web/lib/tldr-content.ts
echo "Rolled back to pre-exploration state"
```

### 5.2 Sync Changes
```bash
br update bd-w8iz --status done
br sync --flush-only
```

### 5.3 Final Verification
- [ ] All tests pass
- [ ] TypeScript compiles without errors
- [ ] Content matches verified tool capabilities
- [ ] Synergies are reciprocal (cm->cass and cass->cm)
- [ ] No broken links or references","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-26T20:01:14.505672108Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-26T21:31:37.021485698Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-zhdi","title":"Shell tab completion for acfs command","description":"## Overview
Add shell tab completion for the `acfs` command (bash and zsh).

## Current Problem
- Users must remember all subcommands
- No discovery mechanism for available options
- Typos in command names cause errors

## Proposed Completion
```bash
$ acfs <TAB>
doctor    install   newproj   status    update

$ acfs doctor --<TAB>
--fix     --json    --quiet   --verbose

$ acfs install <TAB>
all       core      dev       shell     # module names from manifest
```

## Implementation Details
1. Generate completions from manifest.yaml at install time
2. Support both bash and zsh
3. Install to standard locations:
   - Bash: ~/.local/share/bash-completion/completions/acfs
   - Zsh: ~/.local/share/zsh/site-functions/_acfs
4. Source automatically via shell rc files

## Completion Sources
- Static: command names (doctor, install, update, etc.)
- Dynamic: module names from manifest.yaml
- Dynamic: tool names for granular install

## Installation
```bash
# Added to install.sh
acfs_install_completions() {
  local shell="$1"
  case "$shell" in
    bash) install_bash_completions ;;
    zsh)  install_zsh_completions ;;
  esac
}
```

## Test Plan
- [ ] Test bash completion for all commands
- [ ] Test zsh completion for all commands
- [ ] Test dynamic module name completion

@@ -0,0 +1,99 @@
+/**
+ * VPS provider data for the comparison table in the wizard.
+ *
+ * Prices are as of early 2026 and should be reviewed periodically.
+ * Provider data is separated from UI so it's easy to update without
+ * touching component code.
+ *
+ * @see bd-w8fx
+ */
+
+export interface VPSPlan {
+  /** Plan name as shown by the provider */
+  name: string;
+  /** RAM in GB */
+  ramGB: number;
+  /** Number of virtual CPUs */
+  vCPU: number;
+  /** Storage in GB */
+  storageGB: number;
+  /** Monthly price in USD (no-commitment) */
+  priceUSD: number;
+}
+
+export interface VPSProvider {
+  id: string;
+  name: string;
+  /** One-line description */
+  tagline: string;
+  /** URL to VPS product page */
+  url: string;
+  /** Recommended plan tier for 64GB or best available */
+  recommended: VPSPlan;
+  /** Budget plan tier for 48GB or best available */
+  budget: VPSPlan;
+  /** Typical activation time */
+  activationTime: string;
+  /** Key differentiator */
+  bestFor: string;
+  /** Data center regions available */
+  regions: string;
+  /** Whether this provider is our top recommendation */
+  isTopPick?: boolean;
+  /** Additional notes */
+  note?: string;
+}
+
+export const VPS_PROVIDERS: VPSProvider[] = [
+  {
+    id: "contabo",
+    name: "Contabo",
+    tagline: "Best specs-to-price ratio",
+    url: "https://contabo.com/en-us/vps/",
+    recommended: {
+      name: "Cloud VPS 50",
+      ramGB: 64,
+      vCPU: 16,
+      storageGB: 400,
+      priceUSD: 56,
+    },
+    budget: {
+      name: "Cloud VPS 40",
+      ramGB: 48,
+      vCPU: 12,
+      storageGB: 300,
+      priceUSD: 36,
+    },
+    activationTime: "Minutes (up to ~1 hr)",
+    bestFor: "Best value overall",
+    regions: "US, EU, Asia, AU",
+    isTopPick: true,
+    note: "US datacenter pricing includes ~$10/mo surcharge",
+  },
+  {
+    id: "ovh",
+    name: "OVH",
+    tagline: "Polished interface, fast activation",
+    url: "https://us.ovhcloud.com/vps/",
+    recommended: {
+      name: "VPS-5",
+      ramGB: 64,
+      vCPU: 16,
+      storageGB: 640,
+      priceUSD: 40,
+    },
+    budget: {
+      name: "VPS-4",
+      ramGB: 48,
+      vCPU: 12,
+      storageGB: 480,
+      priceUSD: 26,
+    },
+    activationTime: "Minutes",
+    bestFor: "Lowest 64GB price",
+    regions: "US, EU, CA, Asia",
+  },
+];
+
+/** Date the pricing data was last verified */
+export const PRICING_LAST_UPDATED = "2026-01";
